Please read carefully, and make a note of the following special cases and other items:

1. General Technical Electives. The 3 credits may be any upper-level course (300 level or higher) from the math, engineering, and basic science disciplines whose courses start with the following preﬁxes and who do not appear on the list of unacceptable courses available from the Undergraduate Studies Ofﬁce: AMSC, BCHM, BIOE, BSCI, CHEM, CMSC, ENAE, ENCE, ENCH, ENEE, ENES, ENFP, ENMA, ENME, ENNU, ENRE, MATH, PHYS, and STAT. The most up-to-date list of approved and unacceptable courses will always be available from the Undergraduate Studies Ofﬁce and on the ECE website. The 3 credits may be any upper-level course (300 level or higher) whose preﬁx is not given in the list above, assuming that the student: a. completes the application to allow the course to count as a professional goals, and

c. receives signed approval of the Associate Chair for
